title: Legacy Exploits
_note: Info-only reference page covering legacy Wii exploits that are largely superseded by modern methods like str2hax, LetterBomb, and FlashHax.

steps.title: What are legacy exploits?
steps.body:
Before str2hax, LetterBomb, and FlashHax became the standard ways to hack a Wii, there were many other exploits — each tied to a specific game disc, a specific System Menu version, or specific hardware. Most of these are **obsolete** for modern hacking because they require a rare game disc, an old System Menu, or hardware that's hard to find.

This page lists them for reference. If you own the required game or have an older Wii, some of these still work — but the recommended guides on this site use the exploits that work on every 4.3 Wii without needing a rare disc.

Unless you specifically need one of these (e.g. you're restoring a Wii that can't run the modern exploits), use the main guides instead.

steps.title: Smash Stack
steps.body:
**Game:** Super Smash Bros Brawl
**SD card:** 2GB or less
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

Smash Stack works by loading a custom `private` folder from an SD card through Super Smash Bros Brawl's stage builder. It was one of the most widely used exploits and works on every Wii, but requires the Brawl disc.

Place the exploit files on a 2GB or smaller SD card, insert it, launch Brawl → Stage Builder, and the exploit triggers automatically.

steps.title: Twilight Hack
steps.body:
**Game:** The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
**Versions:** Up to System Menu 3.4

The original Wii homebrew exploit. It uses a specially crafted save file for Twilight Princess that triggers a buffer overflow when loaded. Requires an older System Menu (3.4 or below) and the Twilight Princess disc.

Copy the hacked save to your SD card, launch the game, talk to the postman in the first area, and the exploit loads the Homebrew Channel installer.

steps.title: Indiana Pwns
steps.body:
**Game:** LEGO Indiana Jones (original, not the sequel)
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

Uses a buffer overflow in LEGO Indiana Jones through a custom save file. Works on any System Menu version as long as you have the LEGO Indiana Jones disc.

Copy the exploit save to your SD card, launch LEGO Indiana Jones → select the hacked save, and the exploit runs.

steps.title: Bathaxx
steps.body:
**Game:** LEGO Batman
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

Identical approach to Indiana Pwns but for LEGO Batman. Another game-disc save exploit that works on any System Menu version.

Copy the hacked save to your SD card, launch LEGO Batman → load the exploit save.

steps.title: Return of the Jodi
steps.body:
**Game:** LEGO Star Wars (the original, not the Complete Saga)
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

Yet another save-based exploit, this time using LEGO Star Wars. Same approach as the other LEGO game exploits.

Copy the hacked save to your SD card, launch LEGO Star Wars → load the exploit save.

steps.title: Eri HaKawai
steps.body:
**Game:** Tales of Symphonia
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

Save-based exploit using Tales of Symphonia on the Wii. (Tales of Symphonia was originally a GameCube game but the Wii can play it.)

Copy the hacked save to your SD card, launch Tales of Symphonia → load the exploit save.

steps.title: Yu-Gi-OWNED! / Yu-Gi-Vah
steps.body:
**Game:** Yu-Gi-Oh 5D's Wheelie Breakers
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

A save-based exploit using the underrated (and unfortunately not great) Yu-Gi-Oh 5D's Wheelie Breakers game for the Wii. Not many people have this disc, but if you do, it works.

Copy the hacked save to your SD card, launch the game → load the exploit save.

steps.title: szsHaxx
steps.body:
**Game:** Mario Kart Wii
**SD card:** 2GB or less
**Versions:** All System Menu versions

szsHaxx uses a custom track file loaded through Mario Kart Wii's track editor. It doesn't require a save file — instead it loads a hacked `.szs` file from the SD card. Requires a 2GB or smaller SD card.

Place the hacked track file on your SD card, launch Mario Kart Wii → Track Editor → select the custom track.

steps.title: Bannerbomb
steps.body:
**Game:** None (System Menu only)
**Versions:** System Menu 3.0–4.2

Bannerbomb was the first exploit that didn't need a game disc. It works by exploiting a vulnerability in how the Wii renders banner animations in the System Menu's SD card menu.

Place the exploit files on an SD card, insert it into the Wii, and navigate to the SD card menu (bottom-left of the Wii Menu). The exploit triggers when the banner loads.

!!This only works on System Menu 3.0 through 4.2!!. If you're on 4.3, use LetterBomb instead.

steps.title: LetterBomb
steps.body:
**Game:** None (System Menu only)
**Versions:** System Menu 4.3 only

LetterBomb is the successor to Bannerbomb for 4.3 Wiis. It exploits a vulnerability in the Wii Message Board to load the HackMii Installer — no game disc needed.

LetterBomb is still a fully supported modern exploit. If you're on 4.3, use **its dedicated guide** instead of any of the legacy exploits above.

**This guide:** `wii/letterbomb.json`

steps.title: FlashHax
steps.body:
**Game:** None (Internet Channel only)
**Versions:** Any System Menu version

FlashHax works through the Wii's Internet Channel browser. It exploits a vulnerability in Flash to load the HackMii Installer — no SD card or game disc needed.

FlashHax is still a fully supported modern exploit. Use **its dedicated guide** if the Internet Channel is installed.

**This guide:** `wii/flashhax.json`

steps.title: Wuphax
steps.body:
**VWii only**

Wuphax is specifically for the **vWii** (the Wii mode on a Wii U). It exploits the Wii U's Wii Mode to install the Homebrew Channel.

This is still a modern exploit for vWii. If you're hacking a Wii U's vWii, use the dedicated vWii guide.

**This guide:** `wii/wuphax.json`
